title: USA World Cup lineup vs Australia: Pulisic out with injury
original_text:
June 18, 2026Updated June 19, 2026, 2:21 p.m. ET
Editor's note: Follow for live updates from USA vs. Australia World Cup showdown!
SEATTLE — Mauricio Pochettino appears to have found a winning formula with his lineup, so it has not changed much.
But there is, of course, one major caveat for the U.S. men's national team head coach heading into Friday's game against Australia.
Christian Pulisic, who starred in the USMNT's 4-1 win over Paraguay to begin the World Cup, left the match at halftime with a left calf injury. After failing to train in full all week, the 27-year-old is out for Friday's game against Australia.
Pochettino has replaced Pulisic in the lineup with Ricardo Pepi, giving the USMNT a two-forward setup against the Socceroos.
Here is who the USA is starting against Australia:
Shop USMNT World Cup tickets
Goalkeeper: Matt Freese
Freese wasn't tested too much against Paraguay and could do little about the lone goal. He'll keep his spot on Friday.
Defenders: Sergiño Dest, Alex Freeman, Tim Ream, Chris Richards, Antonee Robinson
There aren't any changes at defense after a solid display against Paraguay. Ream will have to be careful, though, about getting isolated against any of Australia's speedy attackers in transition. Nestory Irankunda in particular victimized Turkey's back line in a similar situation.
Dest and Robinson will be key to stretching Australia's defense, though whipping in crosses may be difficult against the Socceroos' tall back line.
Midfielders: Malik Tillman, Tyler Adams, Weston McKennie
The team's new "MMA" midfield is clicking at the right time. It will need more of the intricate passing moves we saw against Paraguay in a game where Australia will likely be bunkered in.
Forwards: Ricardo Pepi, Folarin Balogun
Balogun will start again after his double against Paraguay, but he'll be joined by Pepi in the front line.
USMNT XI vs Australia: Freese; A. Robinson, Ream, Richards, Freeman, Dest; McKennie, Adams, Tillman; Pepi, Balogun
The 2026 World Cup is the largest ever, and USA TODAY is going all in with reporters on the ground in all 16 host cities across three countries to cover 48 teams vying for the trophy. Get our World Cup: Extra Time newsletter in your inbox every morning and join our WhatsApp channel to get the latest updates right in your texts.
USA 2026 World Cup Schedule
The USA national team begins the World Cup in Group D and ranked No. 16 in the FIFA World Rankings.
June 12: USA 4-1 Paraguay. SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, Calif.
June 19: USA vs Australia, 3 p.m. ET, Lumen Field, Seattle on FOX
June 25: USA vs Turkey, 10 p.m. ET, Sofi Stadium, Inglewood, Calif. on FOX
Streaming available on Fubo or the Fox One App. Spanish language broadcasts are available on Telemundo and Peacock.
Watch every World Cup game with Fubo
USA World Cup roster
Goalkeepers: Chris Brady (Chicago Fire), Matt Freese (New York City FC), Matt Turner (New England Revolution)
Defenders: Max Arfsten (Columbus Crew), Sergiño Dest (PSV), Alex Freeman (Villarreal), Mark McKenzie (Toulouse), Tim Ream (Charlotte FC), Chris Richards (Crystal Palace), Antonee Robinson (Fulham), Miles Robinson (FC Cincinnati), Joe Scally (Borussia Mönchengladbach), Auston Trusty (Celtic)
Midfielders: Tyler Adams (AFC Bournemouth), Sebastian Berhalter (Vancouver Whitecaps), Weston McKennie (Juventus), Gio Reyna (Borussia Mönchengladbach), Cristian Roldan (Seattle Sounders), Malik Tillman (Bayer Leverkusen)
Forwards: Brenden Aaronson (Leeds United), Folarin Balogun (Monaco), Ricardo Pepi (PSV), Christian Pulisic (AC Milan), Tim Weah (Olympique Marseille), Haji Wright (Coventry City), Alejandro Zendejas (Club América)
Full rosters for 2026 World Cup teams
USA World Cup history
The U.S. national team is back in the World Cup for the 12th time after last qualifying in 2022. The country's best result in the tournament is third place (1930).
How did USA qualify for the World Cup?
USA, ranked No. 16 in the FIFA Men's World Rankings, earned a berth in the 2026 World Cup as hosts.
Where is the USA national team base camp?
Great Park - Irvine, CA.
